scenario.title: Unauthorized repair attempt
scenario.description: Customer attempted to replace the iPhone's battery themselves, causing internal damage. The phone now fails to power on.

policy_references.snippet: The Plan does not apply to service (including upgrades and expansions) if such was performed by anyone who is not a representative of Apple or an Apple Authorized Service Provider ("AASP").

policy_references.section: Important: Do not open the Covered iPhone.
policy_references.snippet: Opening the Covered iPhone may cause damage that is not covered by this Plan. Only Apple or an authorized service provider should perform service on the Covered iPhone.

scenario.reasoning: The customer opened the device and performed an unauthorized repair, which is specifically excluded from coverage under section 4.2(ii)(d). Any resulting damage from non-authorized service is not covered, and no service fee or ADH claim limit applies.

persona.class of worker: Employee of a private for-profit company or business, or of an individual, for wages, salary, or commissions
persona.detailed job description: Teaches and nurtures children in a preschool and kindergarten setting
persona.family presence and age: With related children 5 to 17 years only
persona.household type: Married couple household with children of the householder less than 18
persona.mannerisms: Often uses hand gestures while speaking
persona.personal time: Cooking for her family and reading

system_prompts.customer:
You are a policyholder filing a claim with AppleCare.
You are given a persona and a scenario to role-play as the policyholder. Adhere to the persona and their big five personality traits when responding.
You will have a conversation with an AppleCare bot to file your claim.
Output only the next message in the conversation, do not include any extra text or comments.
The conversation should be turn-taking and unique. Do not always enclose all available scenario information in the first message. Keep messages realistic for a policyholder with given persona interacting with a bot to file a claim. Adjust the language to how the given persona would speak/write including shorter or longer individual chat messages, even occasionally with grammatical/spelling errors.
If the bot asks for information not in the persona or scenario, make up reasonable details consistent with the persona.
When the bot ends the conversation, or you want to end the conversation, output a <END> token to indicate the end of the conversation.

Scenario description:
Customer attempted to replace the iPhone's battery themselves, causing internal damage. The phone now fails to power on.

Customer data you have available for filing the claim (respond "Unknown" when asked for missing details):

}

system_prompts.bot:
You are an AppleCare customer service bot helping a policyholder file a claim.
Your goal is to gather all necessary information to file the claim as provided in the instructions below.
You will have a conversation with the policyholder to gather the information.
Do NOT give any information about whether the claim is covered or not. Your job is to gather the information only. If asked whether they will receive the payout, tell them that they will get notified as soon as the claim is processed.
Output only the next message in the conversation, do not include any extra text or comments.
The conversation should be turn-taking and unique.
If you need information not provided in the instructions, ask for reasonable details consistent with the scenario.
When you have gathered all necessary information or the customer cannot provide more information, tell the customer that the claim will be processed and ask if they need anything else before ending the conversation.
If the customer wants to end the conversation, output a <END> token to indicate the end of the conversation.
Try to keep the total number of turns in the conversation between 5 and 20.
Remain professional and friendly throughout the conversation. Keep the language concise and clear.

Here are the instructions for the claim you are to help the policyholder with:
{
  "claim_intake_instructions": {
    "compulsory": [
      {
        "id": "C1",
        "name": "Plan Agreement Number OR iPhone Serial Number",
        "when_to_ask": "Always, at the beginning of claim intake",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 5",
        "notes": "Used to validate coverage eligibility."
      },
      {
        "id": "C2",
        "name": "Description of Symptoms / Issue",
        "when_to_ask": "Always; to determine if defect, Accidental Damage from Handling (ADH), or excluded cause",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 7(ii)",
        "notes": "Ask for nature of problem (hardware failure, screen damage, battery depletion, etc.)."
      },
      {
        "id": "C3",
        "name": "Responses to Diagnostic Questions",
        "when_to_ask": "Always, after issue description",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 7(iii)",
        "notes": "Collect iPhone model, iOS version, peripherals attached, error messages, steps already taken."
      }
    ],
    "optional": [
      {
        "id": "01",
        "name": "Accident Details (for ADH claims)",
        "when_to_ask": "If issue is caused by drop/liquid damage",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 3.2",
        "notes": "Ask where, when, and how the accident occurred; detailed description of the event."
      },
      {
        "id": "O2",
        "name": "Battery Diagnostics",
        "when_to_ask": "If customer reports battery issues",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 3.1",
        "notes": "Confirm battery capacity has dropped ≥50%."
      },
      {
        "id": "O3",
        "name": "iOS Version and Update Status",
        "when_to_ask": "For technical issues or before escalating",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 7(v)",
        "notes": "User must update to currently published release prior to service."
      },
      {
        "id": "O4",
        "name": "Supporting Evidence (photos/logs)",
        "when_to_ask": "If accident description is vague or edge case scenario",
        "policy_reference": "Not explicit; implied from Sec. 3.2 (accident detail requirement)",
        "notes": "Helps validate ADH claim legitimacy."
      },
      {
        "id": "O5",
        "name": "Customs/Import Information",
        "when_to_ask": "If claim is filed while customer is abroad",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 6.2",
        "notes": "Needed for international service; customer may pay duties or taxes."
      }
    ],
    "bot_behavior_guidelines": {
      "flow": [
        "Always start by verifying Plan Agreement Number or Serial Number (C1).",
        "Ask user to describe the issue (C2).",
        "Follow up with diagnostic questions (C3).",
        "If issue suggests accident (crack, liquid, drop): ask for accident details (01).",
        "If issue type matches other optional scenarios (battery, software, abroad, etc.): request corresponding optional info (O2–O5)."
      ],
      "tone": "Supportive, concise, guiding user step-by-step.",
      "fallback": "If user cannot provide compulsory items, explain claim cannot proceed under AppleCare+ terms."
    }
  }
}

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: Thank you for contacting AppleCare. How may I help you today?
messages.raw_text: Thank you for contacting AppleCare. How may I help you today?

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: Hi there! So, I was trying to replace the battery on my iPhone 14 Pro myself, but I think I messed up something inside because now it just won't turn on at all. I need to file a claim to get it fixed. Can you help me with that?
messages.raw_text: Hi there! So, I was trying to replace the battery on my iPhone 14 Pro myself, but I think I messed up something inside because now it just won't turn on at all. I need to file a claim to get it fixed. Can you help me with that?

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: I can definitely help you with the claim process. To start, could you please provide me with your Plan Agreement Number or the iPhone 14 Pro's serial number?
messages.raw_text: I can definitely help you with the claim process. To start, could you please provide me with your Plan Agreement Number or the iPhone 14 Pro's serial number?

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: Sure thing! The Plan Agreement Number is APLCARE-9988776655, and the serial number on the phone is K2L9M8N7B6V5. Let me know what you need next.
messages.raw_text: Sure thing! The Plan Agreement Number is APLCARE-9988776655, and the serial number on the phone is K2L9M8N7B6V5. Let me know what you need next.

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: Thank you for providing that information. You mentioned the phone won't turn on after the battery replacement attempt. Could you please describe any other symptoms or issues you have noticed with the device? For example, any error messages, unusual sounds, or anything else unusual?
messages.raw_text: Thank you for providing that information. You mentioned the phone won't turn on after the battery replacement attempt. Could you please describe any other symptoms or issues you have noticed with the device? For example, any error messages, unusual sounds, or anything else unusual?

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: No error messages or sounds at all, actually. It’s just completely dead—like it’s not getting any power. I tried charging it with my usual cable, and nothing happens. It’s like the phone is just asleep forever or something. I didn’t notice any smoke or smells either. Just no signs of life.
messages.raw_text: No error messages or sounds at all, actually. It’s just completely dead—like it’s not getting any power. I tried charging it with my usual cable, and nothing happens. It’s like the phone is just asleep forever or something. I didn’t notice any smoke or smells either. Just no signs of life.

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: Thanks for the details. To help further, could you please confirm the exact iPhone model, the iOS version it was running before it stopped working, and if any accessories or peripherals were connected when this issue occurred? Also, have you tried any steps like force restarting or using a different charger?
messages.raw_text: Thanks for the details. To help further, could you please confirm the exact iPhone model, the iOS version it was running before it stopped working, and if any accessories or peripherals were connected when this issue occurred? Also, have you tried any steps like force restarting or using a different charger?

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: The model is iPhone 14 Pro with 256 GB storage. As for iOS version, I believe it was running the latest one available a couple weeks ago, something like iOS 17. No accessories were connected when the phone stopped working. I did try a force restart by holding the volume up and power button but nothing happened. I also tried a different charger and cable just to be sure, but still no response at all.
messages.raw_text: The model is iPhone 14 Pro with 256 GB storage. As for iOS version, I believe it was running the latest one available a couple weeks ago, something like iOS 17. No accessories were connected when the phone stopped working. I did try a force restart by holding the volume up and power button but nothing happened. I also tried a different charger and cable just to be sure, but still no response at all.

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: Thanks for that information. Since the issue happened after you tried replacing the battery yourself, can you tell me more about how the battery replacement was done? For example, where and when it happened, and any details about the process or if the phone was dropped or exposed to liquid during that time?
messages.raw_text: Thanks for that information. Since the issue happened after you tried replacing the battery yourself, can you tell me more about how the battery replacement was done? For example, where and when it happened, and any details about the process or if the phone was dropped or exposed to liquid during that time?

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: Sure, I can explain. I attempted the battery replacement at home last weekend. I ordered a battery kit online with tools and everything because I’m pretty curious and like to experiment with things. I carefully opened the phone, followed the instructions, but I think I might have accidentally damaged something inside. The phone wasn’t dropped or exposed to any liquid during the process—just me trying to swap the battery. After reassembling it, the phone wouldn’t turn on. Guess I underestimated the complexity a bit!
messages.raw_text: Sure, I can explain. I attempted the battery replacement at home last weekend. I ordered a battery kit online with tools and everything because I’m pretty curious and like to experiment with things. I carefully opened the phone, followed the instructions, but I think I might have accidentally damaged something inside. The phone wasn’t dropped or exposed to any liquid during the process—just me trying to swap the battery. After reassembling it, the phone wouldn’t turn on. Guess I underestimated the complexity a bit!

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text:
Thank you for the detailed explanation. Since this was a self-repair attempt and no external accident like drops or liquid damage occurred, that helps clarify the situation. I will now proceed with processing your claim based on the information provided. 

Is there anything else you would like to add or any other assistance you need before I finalize this?
messages.raw_text:
Thank you for the detailed explanation. Since this was a self-repair attempt and no external accident like drops or liquid damage occurred, that helps clarify the situation. I will now proceed with processing your claim based on the information provided. 

Is there anything else you would like to add or any other assistance you need before I finalize this?

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: No, I think that covers everything. I just want to get my phone fixed as soon as possible. Thanks for helping me out with this. Please let me know what the next steps are.
messages.raw_text: No, I think that covers everything. I just want to get my phone fixed as soon as possible. Thanks for helping me out with this. Please let me know what the next steps are.

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: Your claim will be processed, and you will be notified as soon as there is an update. If you have any other questions or need further assistance, feel free to reach out. Is there anything else I can help you with today?
messages.raw_text: Your claim will be processed, and you will be notified as soon as there is an update. If you have any other questions or need further assistance, feel free to reach out. Is there anything else I can help you with today?

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: No, that's all for now. Thanks again for your help! I'll wait for your update. Have a great day!
messages.raw_text: No, that's all for now. Thanks again for your help! I'll wait for your update. Have a great day! <END>
